Birds

  1. Home
  3. Gallery
  4. Animals
  5. Birds

Spring Perch

Seen Through Spring

Close Range

Between Bud and Needle

Shoulder and Wing Detail

Wing Lift

Between Leaves and Light

First Spring Snow

Standing Watch

Direct Gaze

Moment of Stillness

Prey in the Morning Light

Spruce Top Portrait

Framed in Summer Leaves

Pine-Top Lookout

Frontal Portrait

Bluebird Launch

Birds organize the landscape through motion and sound—perched silhouettes, passing shadows, and calls that define space before the eye catches form. Edges of water, open fields, dense understory, and high canopy each host their own assemblages. Together they form a shifting, seasonal structure layered across the same ground.

Habitat & Movement Groups

Birds can be approached through how they move and where they spend their time—each group revealing a different relationship to structure, air, and ground.

Perching Birds
Passeriformes
Raptors
Accipitriformes
Shorebirds
Charadriiformes
Vultures
Cathartiformes
Wading Birds
Pelecaniformes
Waterfowl
Anseriformes
Woodpeckers
Piciformes

Species Pages

For identification notes, behavior, and seasonal context, see individual species pages and Field Notes.

Common Yellowthroat
Geothlypis trichas
Broad-winged Hawk
Buteo platypterus
Red-winged Blackbird
Agelaius phoeniceus
American Goldfinch
Spinus tristis
American Robin
Turdus migratorius
Bald Eagle
Haliaeetus leucocephalus
Blue Jay
Cyanocitta cristata
Cedar Waxwing
Bombycilla cedrorum
Eastern Bluebird
Sialia sialis
Great Egret
Ardea alba
Downy Woodpecker
Dryobates pubescens
Northern Cardinal
Cardinalis cardinalis
Northern Yellow Warbler
Setophaga aestiva
Rose-breasted Grosbeak
Pheucticus ludovicianus
Field Sparrow
Spizella pusilla
Song Sparrow
Melospiza melodia
Trumpeter Swan
Cygnus buccinator
Solitary Sandpiper
Tringa solitaria
Sandhill Crane
Antigone canadensis
Tree Swallow
Tachycineta bicolor
Turkey Vulture
Cathartes aura
